categories: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions

faqs.question: What is the Vicharanashala internship?
faqs.answer: A two-month, full-time engagement at the Vicharanashala Lab, a research lab at IIT Ropar. You will work on a real open-source project under a mentor, after a short training phase tailored to where you already are. The internship is free — we do not charge, and the work is real.

faqs.question: What is VINS?
faqs.answer: VINS is the Vicharanashala Internship — an online programme open to anyone who clears our interview. The work is real open-source contribution under a mentor, the certificate is from the Vicharanashala Lab for Education Design at IIT Ropar, and the programme itself is free (we charge nothing). There is no stipend. If you are seeing a yellow VINS panel on your result page, you are selected.

faqs.question: What are the phases of VINS, and what do the badges mean?
faqs.answer: VINS is structured as four phases, each marked by a badge: Bronze (Phase 1) — a short training period at the start, planned around what you already know; your mentor may skip it if you're already comfortable with the basics. Silver (Phase 2) — the main work, contributing to a real open-source project under a Vicharanashala mentor; finishing Bronze and Silver completes your internship and earns the certificate. Gold (Phase 3) — a recognition awarded during Silver if your contribution stands on its own as a meaningful feature, not just a small fix. Platinum (Phase 4) — a standing invitation to visit the lab any time during the year after your internship ends, with a small visit stipend to help with travel. Most interns finish at Bronze + Silver, which is exactly what the certificate is for; Gold and Platinum are extras you can earn through your work.

faqs.question: Who is the internship for? Are alumni eligible?
faqs.answer: The internship is for currently-enrolled students at any college or university — undergraduate, postgraduate, or doctoral. The NOC requirement reflects this: it is institutional consent that you can commit your time to the internship. Candidates who have already graduated and are not currently enrolled in any programme are not eligible for this cycle. If you re-enrol later (higher studies, etc.), you are welcome to apply again in a future cycle.

faqs.question: Is this the same as IIT Ropar's official Summer Research Internship?
faqs.answer: No. Summership 2026 is a VLED Lab initiative. The certificate is issued by the Vicharanashala Lab for Education Design, not centrally by the institute. IIT Ropar runs a separate institutional summer research internship through its own office, and Summership 2026 should not be represented as equivalent to that programme.

faqs.question: I have to attend my class tomorrow/today/some day — can I take leave?
faqs.answer: Leave is not permitted. If you are also attending classes or exams, you will be relieved from the internship immediately and will need to join the next batch when it starts.

faqs.question: When can I start?
faqs.answer: You can start any time in 2026 — VINS is flexible on the start date — but your internship must finish by 31 December 2026 (non-negotiable); your end date (start + 2 months, with up to 1 month grace) must land on or before that date. It is strongly recommended that you start as soon as possible, because: (1) Cohort networking — the batch goes through Bronze together with peer discussions and lasting connections, while later in the year the cohort disperses and late starters are largely solo; (2) TA support is concentrated in May–July, when TAs are full-time, and thinner afterward as they return to college work; (3) Training rolls out with the cohort, with discussion around it, rather than as a static document. If starting now is genuinely impossible (exams, other unavoidable commitments), you can begin later and still complete and earn the certificate, but the cohort effect and support will be lighter and the December cap leaves no room for slippage.

faqs.question: How long is the internship?
faqs.answer: Two months from your chosen start date, with an optional one-month grace period if you need it. The end date must land on or before 31 December 2026.

faqs.question: Can I start in July, August or later if I have exams now?
faqs.answer: Yes — but only if your exams genuinely make an earlier start impossible. Wait until your exams are done, then opt in and start; do not attempt to juggle the internship with ongoing exams. Make sure your chosen start date plus 2 months (or 3 with grace) lands on or before 31 December 2026.

faqs.question: Can I start with the cohort and take a relaxation during my exam window?
faqs.answer: No, this is not an arrangement that is offered. VINS is a full-attention internship — six to ten hours a day, sometimes more — and splitting that with college exams damages both sides: the project loses momentum, the exams suffer, and the mentor invests in someone who can only half-engage. If your exams fall inside the cohort duration, defer your start to after your exams end, opt in then, and run the internship at full attention; the certificate and project pathway are the same. Note on consequences: if it is later learned that a candidate was sitting college exams during their internship period, the programme reserves the right to terminate the internship or withhold the certificate at any time, including after the internship has otherwise been completed.

faqs.question: Can I take leave or get an exemption during the internship for an exam scheduled in June?
faqs.answer: The attendance rule is firm — the 55-day continuous window is a non-negotiable part of the internship, and no exemption can be offered for an exam during this period, because split attention genuinely damages both exam preparation and internship work.

faqs.question: Are orientation session recordings shared with interns, and can project or group assignments be changed after watching them?
faqs.answer: Recordings of the sessions will not be provided. However, an abridged version of a talk or session may be shared if considered important — this is not guaranteed for every session.

faqs.question: What dates do I put on the NOC?
faqs.answer: Default: your chosen start date through your start + 2 months (with up to 1 month grace), ensuring the end date is on or before 31 December 2026 — pick the earliest start date you can realistically make, since the May–July summer window is the main cohort. If the NOC will be signed on a specific later date, pick a start date after the signature date.

faqs.question: Who can sign the NOC?
faqs.answer: Any authorised signatory at your college: HOD, Acting HOD (during holidays), Principal, Dean, Director, or Training & Placement Officer. For dual-degree students, either institution can sign — pick whichever is easier. For IITM BS Online Degree (standalone) students, any officer from the BS office can sign.

faqs.question: When do I submit the NOC? Is the deadline hard?
faqs.answer: There is no specific calendar cut-off date for uploading the NOC, but your internship cannot formally begin until your official institutional NOC has been uploaded and validated. Submit your signed NOC as early as possible to join the current summer cohort. You can technically upload your NOC and start later in the year, but this is strongly discouraged — your mentor may already be busy with other work, you won't network properly with the rest of the cohort, and the cohort + TA support that make the internship good are concentrated during the summer window.

faqs.question: What format should I use? Do I need to design it myself?
faqs.answer: No — a printable NOC format is provided. Once your result is out and you log in to samagama.in, you will see a Download blank NOC button on your dashboard. Take a printout, get it physically signed and stamped by your authorised signatory, scan it, and upload the signed PDF using the Upload signed NOC button (also on the dashboard). You do not need to draft anything yourself, and you do not need college letterhead — the provided format is the canonical layout.

faqs.question: What if my college / Program Chair gives me an NOC in their own format?
faqs.answer: A college's own NOC format is acceptable as long as all required entries are present: the signing authority's (HOD / Dean / Program Chair / Principal) handwritten signature (most important item); the signing authority's name, designation, official email address, and phone number (cross-checked to verify the signature is genuine); your full name and the internship period (start and end dates); and your signature. If your college's format has no place for your signature, sign clearly and prominently anywhere on the document before uploading. With those entries present, you do not need to switch to the printable format — an NOC missing any of them is incomplete and will be returned for correction.

faqs.question: Does it need to be signed by hand?
faqs.answer: Yes. Three things are required, all on the provided NOC format: the authorised signatory's handwritten signature; the institutional rubber stamp / seal applied in the designated area; and the signatory's email address filled in the designated field (used to cross-check that the signature is genuine). Digital signatures are not accepted — the only path is a physically-signed printout uploaded by you from the dashboard.

faqs.question: Can my HOD email the NOC instead of uploading it?
faqs.answer: No. Your NOC must be uploaded by you, the student, from your dashboard — NOCs are no longer accepted by email. An earlier email-forward path (where the HOD emailed the NOC) has been retired, and NOCs emailed by you or your HOD will not be processed. The only accepted way is to download the format, get it signed by the appropriate authority, and upload the signed PDF yourself from your dashboard. If your college gives you a signed NOC in their own format, that is fine, but you still upload it yourself as a PDF.

faqs.question: How do I download and upload the NOC?
faqs.answer: Both happen on your dashboard at samagama.in once your result is out. The NOC section with two buttons appears in three places (all backed by the same endpoints): a compact pill in the dark header bar at the top of every screen; a standalone NOC card on the dashboard between the Results card and the Talk-to-Yaksha button; and a NOC section at the bottom of your full Result message. The two buttons are 'Download blank NOC' (saves the printable PDF) and 'Upload signed NOC (PDF)' (opens a file picker; PDF must be at most 1 MB, with confirmation appearing on the same card once received). The chat surface no longer carries any NOC affordance. If you can't see the buttons, make sure you're logged in as the email that received the result and that your result has been released.

faqs.question: What if my NOC is not formally verified?
faqs.answer: NOC verification typically takes anywhere between an hour and one full working day from upload. The offer letter is issued automatically once your signed institutional NOC is uploaded and validated — there is no faster route (the earlier self-declaration / provisional-offer option was retired on 2026-05-27 and is no longer accepted). Upload your signed NOC as early as you can so your start is not delayed.

faqs.question: My online course (Masai, NPTEL, Coursera, etc.) won't issue an NOC. What do I do?
faqs.answer: The internship is open only to candidates currently enrolled in a full-time degree programme at a recognised college or university — online-only courses (Masai Institute, NPTEL/MOOC enrolments, Coursera, Udacity, bootcamps, etc.) do not by themselves make a candidate eligible. If you are concurrently enrolled in a full-time degree programme alongside the online course, obtain a No Due / No Objection certificate from that college (department, Dean's office, or Principal) and upload it via the NOC upload flow. If your only current academic engagement is the online course, the internship is not open to you this cycle — you are welcome to apply again once enrolled in a full-time programme.

faqs.question: My HOD/college official wants written confirmation before signing my NOC. What do I show them?
faqs.answer: Your selection is already confirmed the moment your yellow VINS (or green VISE) result panel appears on your samagama.in dashboard — that is the official confirmation of your selection, and is what your HOD should sign your NOC on the basis of. There is no separate written confirmation letter or other proof-of-selection document issued before the NOC step, and none can be sent on request (the selection-confirmation letter and self-declaration/provisional-offer route have both been discontinued). The offer letter is issued only after your signed NOC is uploaded and validated, so it is not available beforehand. If your college won't sign without something in hand, show them your VINS result panel as evidence of selection.

faqs.question: Can Prof. Sudarshan Iyengar or a faculty member from IIT Ropar sign my NOC for the internship?
faqs.answer: Your NOC must be signed by an authorised signatory at the institution where you are enrolled as a student — such as your HOD, Dean, Principal, or Training & Placement Officer. Sudarshan Iyengar is a faculty member at IIT Ropar and is not the authorised signatory for the IIT Ropar/Masai online AIML programme, and cannot sign your NOC in a personal capacity. On eligibility: the internship is open to candidates currently enrolled in a UG/PG/Diploma programme at a recognised college or university; an online-only certification course (even if offered jointly with an IIT) does not meet that requirement on its own. If you are concurrently enrolled in a full-time degree programme elsewhere, obtain the NOC from the authorised signatory there. If your only current academic enrolment is the IIT Ropar/Masai online programme, you are not eligible for this internship cycle.

faqs.question: How do I know I am selected?
faqs.answer: If you can see your yellow VINS result panel on samagama.in, you are selected. There is no separate selection step or confirmation email.

faqs.question: How do I opt into VINS?
faqs.answer: Tell Yaksha in the chat: "I want to take up the online internship without stipend." Yaksha will confirm. Opting in is the selection — no separate confirmation email is sent at that stage.

faqs.question: When do I get the offer letter?
faqs.answer: Your offer letter is issued automatically once you upload your signed institutional NOC (and have confirmed your start and end dates on the dashboard) and it is validated — typically within an hour to one full working day of upload. There is a single offer letter on Vicharanashala letterhead; once your NOC is validated it is the operative offer for your college/employer records (the earlier self-declaration/provisional-offer 'fast path' was retired on 2026-05-27 and is no longer available). The offer letter lives on your dashboard at samagama.in, not in your email — a notification appears in the Announcements section when it is issued. Log in and click 'Download Offer Letter' from the Offer Letter card on your dashboard. If you don't see it, do a hard refresh and log back in, or raise #escalate in Yaksha chat.

faqs.question: Will I get a certificate?
faqs.answer: Yes — every intern who completes the internship gets a certificate from Vicharanashala, IIT Ropar. The internship is genuinely demanding; candidates who drop out mid-way do not get a certificate, because finishing means something and the bar is high.

faqs.question: How do I confirm my internship dates?
faqs.answer: Once you have opted into VINS in the chat with Yaksha, log in to samagama.in. On the dashboard, you will see a yellow card titled 'Confirm your internship dates' with two date pickers pre-filled with sensible defaults for the current cohort. If those work, hit 'Save dates'; otherwise edit them to your earliest realistic start — your end must be on or before 31 December 2026. A green confirmation appears once saved, and you can edit any time from the same card. Order doesn't matter — you can save your dates before or after uploading your NOC. The dates you enter must match the internship period your HOD signs off on in your NOC; if you need to change the period later, edit the dates on the same card and upload a fresh NOC matching the new period.

faqs.question: I am a minor/major in AI student, can I join the programme? I don't need a NOC as I am from IIT Ropar
faqs.answer: Minor/Major in AI course from IIT Ropar is a certification course, and there will be a different track of internship equivalent to it — write in separately for this. To be part of this internship programme you should be a registered student in a UG/PG programme with some university; the internship is exclusively meant for students, not working professionals.

faqs.question: How do I accept the offer letter?
faqs.answer: Acceptance happens entirely on your dashboard at samagama.in — there is no email reply and nothing to paste or post. The Offer Letter card guides you through three steps, all of which must be complete (within 5 days of your offer being issued) for your offer to be recorded as accepted: (1) Offer letter — download it, sign the Acceptance of Offer block at the bottom, and upload the signed PDF; (2) Terms & Conditions (Participation Agreement) — read each section and tick the box confirming you've understood it; (3) Honor Code — download it, sign the last page, and upload the signed PDF. Each PDF upload is capped at 1 MB; to correct a file, just upload a new copy, which replaces the previous one. The card updates as you complete each step and shows your offer as accepted once all three are done — there is no acceptance email to send. If all three steps are complete and the card hasn't updated after a refresh, log in and type #escalate in the Yaksha chat.

faqs.question: Can I change my internship dates?
faqs.answer: Before the offer letter is issued: yes — open the Confirm Internship Dates card on your dashboard and edit the dates at any time (end date must be on or before 31 December 2026). After the offer letter is issued: no, dates are final and will not be changed. If the confirmed dates don't work for you, follow the Vicharanashala LinkedIn page for announcements about future cohorts. If you updated your NOC to reflect new dates before the offer letter was issued, upload the revised NOC via the dashboard.

faqs.question: My NOC is not ready but my start date is approaching. What do I do?
faqs.answer: Get your signed institutional NOC uploaded as soon as you can. Your start date cannot be honoured until your official NOC is uploaded and validated — the internship formally begins only after that. If your NOC is not in by your chosen start date, your start simply shifts to whenever it is validated. (The earlier self-declaration/provisional-offer option was retired on 2026-05-27 and is no longer accepted, so a signed institutional NOC is the only way forward.)

faqs.question: When does my internship actually begin? Will I receive a notification on the day?
faqs.answer: Your internship begins on the start date you confirmed on the dashboard — the same date printed on your offer letter — provided your official institutional NOC has been uploaded and validated by then. If your validated NOC is not yet in on your start date, your start shifts to the day it is validated. There is no separate 'your internship has begun' email, chat message, or dashboard notification on the day itself — the start date is the start date. On the morning of your start date, log in to samagama.in and Yaksha will guide you through the Day-1 steps of the Bronze phase. If your dashboard appears unchanged, do a hard refresh and re-login; if it still looks the same, type #escalate in the chat.

faqs.question: Can I switch from VINS (online) to VISE (offline) after being selected?
faqs.answer: The two tracks are finalised at the interview stage, and candidates are not moved between them. VISE has a fixed on-campus capacity planned around mentor bandwidth, hostel availability, and stipend allocation — once the shortlist is set, it stays set. VINS is not a consolation track; the project, the mentor, and the certificate are the same as VISE — what differs is the mode (online) and the absence of a fellowship. Many interns find the online format more effective. The best path forward is to confirm your VINS start dates and get your NOC uploaded.

faqs.question: Can I change my internship dates after the offer letter?
faqs.answer: No. Once your offer letter has been issued, the dates you confirmed are final and will not be changed at this stage. If the confirmed dates do not work for you, follow the Vicharanashala LinkedIn page for announcements about future cohorts.

faqs.question: What will I work on?
faqs.answer: A real open-source project from Vicharanashala's portfolio, assigned based on your background and the lab's current needs. Areas range across AI/ML, web development, NLP, computer vision, agriculture-tech (Annam.AI), education-tech (ViBe), and open-source infrastructure. The exact problem is not pre-published; you join knowing the lab will assign the project.

faqs.question: How many hours per day?
faqs.answer: Plan for 6 to 10 hours a day, sometimes more during the build phase. This is a full-time internship for the two-month window; most candidates who drop out are juggling something else, since VINS expects your full attention.

faqs.question: Who is my mentor?
faqs.answer: You will work with the lab's research and engineering team. The exact mentor depends on the project. The model is fluid — you'll get help from a senior researcher one day, a peer the next, and someone else for a different question — that is how real open-source work happens.

faqs.question: Is there a stipend?
faqs.answer: No. The internship is unpaid. Stellar performers may be recognised with a discretionary stipend at the lab's option, but this is not promised or expected.

faqs.question: Do I need my own laptop? Should I preload any software?
faqs.answer: Yes — a personal laptop is required. It's preferred that you bring a laptop running Linux or macOS. If you use Windows, install a terminal that can SSH and run Unix-style commands — Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) is a clean choice, and a tool such as PuTTY also works, since you'll be SSH-ing into machines and using the command line as part of the work. There is no fixed software-preload list — your mentor will guide you on the specific tools needed once your project is assigned.

faqs.question: I am using a different email on GitHub / Zoom / the learning platform. Is that okay?
faqs.answer: No. Your registered email is your sole identifier across all programme platforms — progress tracking, mentor assignment, and certificate issuance are all tied to it. Mismatches between platforms cannot be retroactively corrected, so ensure you use your registered email everywhere from day one.

faqs.question: Why has my mentor not been assigned yet, or contacted me on day 1?
faqs.answer: Mentors are not assigned on day 1 of the internship. You will be assigned a mentor when you move on to the project phase of VINS, which comes later in the timeline. Before that, you must complete the mandatory coursework of the Bronze phase. Once coursework is complete and you are placed on a project, your mentor will reach out. Note: there is no Discord server run for the programme — see the communication channels FAQ for official channels.

faqs.question: What are the official communication channels?
faqs.answer: Official channels only. The Announcements section on samagama.in is how interns are notified — all programme notifications are posted there (no longer sent by email). Log in and check it regularly during working hours; sessions are announced at least 1 hour before they begin. To get help with a question or problem, follow this order: (1) Yaksha chat on samagama.in — the first place to bring any question or problem; (2) Discussion forum — if Yaksha cannot resolve the matter, the link was sent in your sign-up email and is posted in Announcements, used for troubleshooting and discussion with peers and the team; (3) Raise an issue — only if the discussion forum does not resolve it, go to samagama.in/escalation and click 'Raise an issue', describing what was tried at each prior step; a peer intern answers and a senior reviews before the reply reaches you. WhatsApp support is cancelled — there is no WhatsApp troubleshooting group, and not being on WhatsApp puts you at no disadvantage. Unofficial groups are strictly prohibited: creating, joining, or operating any WhatsApp group, Telegram channel, Discord server, or other peer-coordinated space involving interns (or a subset of interns), or contacting another intern through their personal phone number, is against the code of conduct — any reported complaint leads to immediate termination of the internship. You may connect with fellow interns over LinkedIn and email.

faqs.question: My interview is not marked as complete on the dashboard — what do I do?
faqs.answer: A data-sync issue sometimes occurs where the chat session closes but the interview record doesn't update to 'completed.' The team will check your record and manually mark it as complete if needed — you will be unblocked within 1–2 hours. If you don't hear back and the interview continues to be marked incomplete, write to internship@vicharanashala.ai

faqs.question: Does Vicharanashala send a grade report or evaluation to my university for internship credit?
faqs.answer: Vicharanashala does not send formal evaluation or grade reports to universities — that process is between you and your college. The certificate issued upon completion is the document you can submit to your college or placement office for credit; whether your college accepts it and how they translate it into a grade is their decision. If your college specifically requires a structured evaluation form or performance report, raise that with them directly — the programme can provide the certificate and, if earned, a letter of recommendation, but does not have a process for sending grade reports to universities.

faqs.question: Does the Vicharanashala internship certificate specify whether it was completed online or offline?
faqs.answer: The certificate is the same for both tracks. It is issued by Vicharanashala, IIT Ropar, and does not specify whether you completed it online or on campus — it records only that you completed the internship; the mode is not called out separately on the document.

faqs.question: Will the completion certificate be a physical hardcopy or an e-certificate?
faqs.answer: The completion certificate is issued as an e-certificate, downloaded from your dashboard on samagama.in after completing both Bronze and Silver. Physical copies are not printed or mailed. The certificate is digitally signed and authentic, so it cannot be duplicated, and can also be verified from the database using the number on the certificate.

faqs.question: Is there a WhatsApp group for candidates during the internship?
faqs.answer: No. See the official communication channels FAQ (Section 6) for the official channels.

faqs.question: What is Rosetta?
faqs.answer: Rosetta is your internship journal — a 65-day document, one entry per day, every day, for the full duration of Summership 2026. You write in it daily, keep it privately, and submit it at the end of the internship as one of your completion requirements. The name comes from the Rosetta Stone — discovered in 1799, it carried the same text in three scripts and became the key to decoding an ancient language silent for centuries; not because it was grand, but because it was honest and consistent. That is what the journal is meant to be: sixty-five days of honest writing becoming the key to understanding your own experience — what you learned, where you struggled, what shifted in you.

faqs.question: Why does this exist? Is it just busywork?
faqs.answer: No, it exists for two reasons. For you: most people go through an intense experience and carry it without processing it, finishing unable to articulate what they learned or what changed. The journal builds that articulation one day at a time — students who reflect regularly during a programme consistently get more out of it, not because they work harder, but because they understand what they are doing and why. For us: when you submit Rosetta at the end, it gives qualitative insight into your experience that no survey or evaluation can capture, used to understand what worked, what didn't, and how to improve the programme for the next cohort.

faqs.question: What is a "thinking routine"?
faqs.answer: Each day in Rosetta has a thinking routine — a short framework giving your reflection a specific shape, instead of staring at a blank page. Examples: 3-2-1 (3 things you engaged with, 2 questions on your mind, 1 surprise); Muddy/Clear (what is sharp, and what is still foggy); What? So What? Now What? (separate facts from meaning from action). The routines rotate across the 65 days so the journal doesn't feel repetitive or mechanical — some will feel easy, some will make you stop and actually think. You don't need to research or prepare for the routine; just read the description at the top of each day's entry and write.

faqs.question: How do I get my Rosetta journal?
faqs.answer: The Rosetta template is at: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xVqTgKfqP-EWnPvowyw2ME9d-3b_VMbBqx9p8lDQXLc/edit?tab=t.0 — open the link, go to File → Make a copy, save it to your own Google Drive, and rename it 'Rosetta — [Your Name] — Summership 2026'. That copy is yours for the full duration of your internship. Do not write in the shared template; always work in your own copy.

faqs.question: How do I use it day to day?
faqs.answer: (1) Open your Rosetta Google Doc. (2) Scroll to the entry for today's day number. (3) Fill in the date at the top of the entry. (4) Read the thinking routine name and its one-line description. (5) Answer the three prompts in the writing boxes below. (6) Close it and get on with your day. It should take between 10 and 20 minutes — it is not an essay or a report, it is a journal.

faqs.question: How long should each entry be?
faqs.answer: There is no minimum or maximum word count. A good entry is honest and specific — three to five sentences per prompt is usually enough; write more if something genuinely needs unpacking, or less if a day was quiet. What is not acceptable: one-word answers, copy-pasted text, vague non-answers like 'today was productive,' or anything that reads like it was generated by an AI.

faqs.question: What is the one rule?
faqs.answer: Write what is true. Not what sounds impressive, not what you think is wanted, not a polished summary of the day. If you hated today, write that. If you are confused and frustrated, write that. If something clicked, write that. Entries that read like an LLM wrote them will be noticed — the journal only counts as a completion requirement if it is genuinely yours, in your voice, from your actual experience.

faqs.question: Can I use ChatGPT or any AI tool to write my entries?
faqs.answer: No — this is the one firm rule of Rosetta. The journal is a record of your thinking, not a demonstration of what an AI can produce on your behalf. Entries that read as AI-generated will not count toward your completion requirement, and if the entire journal reads this way, it will not be accepted. AI tools may be used for your technical work if permitted in the programme, but not here.

faqs.question: What if I miss a day?
faqs.answer: Fill it in as soon as you can. Write the actual date you are filling it in, not the date of the missed entry, and be honest in the entry about writing it late and why. Do not leave entries blank — a late honest entry is always better than no entry. If you find yourself consistently missing entries, that's worth paying attention to, as it usually means something else is going wrong; use Yaksha in chat or reach out to your scholar.

faqs.question: Will anyone read my journal during the internship?
faqs.answer: No. Your journal will not be accessed during the 65 days — you write it, you keep it, it is yours. The only time it is read is after you submit it at the end of the internship. This is intentional, to let you write freely without feeling observed — the journal is only useful if it is honest, and only honest if you are not performing for an audience.

faqs.question: Can the prompts change mid-internship?
faqs.answer: Occasionally a prompt for a specific day may be updated based on what's happening in the cohort — a major milestone, a collective challenge, or something the team wants to address directly. When this happens, it will be announced in the Announcements section on samagama.in before that day begins. Check Announcements before filling any entry where a change has been announced; otherwise use the prompt as written in your document.

faqs.question: How do I submit Rosetta at the end?
faqs.answer: On or before Day 65, share your Rosetta Google Doc with the programme coordinator's email address (shared during wrap-up week), with sharing permission set to Viewer. Make sure your name is in the document title ('Rosetta — [Your Name] — Summership 2026'), all 65 entries are filled in, and your cover page has your name, product, and team filled in. Rosetta submission is one of the required criteria for receiving your internship certificate — an incomplete or AI-generated journal will not be accepted.

faqs.question: I have a question about Rosetta that is not answered here. What do I do?
faqs.answer: Ask Yaksha first. If Yaksha cannot answer it, escalate to your programme coordinator. Do not sit on a question — the journal works best when you start it right.

faqs.question: My college requires a written confirmation that the internship is self-paced and will not clash with college classes — what document can I share with them?
faqs.answer: This is not a self-paced internship, but a very rigorous one which is time-demanding. It is not permitted for one to be part of any other activity during this period.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: I've already completed a course (Vinternship, Pinternship, MERN, or AI) with you in an earlier cohort — am I exempt from repeating it?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you completed the Vinternship, Pinternship, MERN, or AI course in an earlier cohort, you don't have to repeat it — submit the exemption form and you'll be exempted from that course. What counts as 'completed'? A course is treated as complete when your ViBe progress is above 95% (the bar is set just below 100% so you're not chasing the last percentage point); this is verified against your ViBe record before confirming. If your progress is below 95%, you cannot be exempted and must finish the remaining portion on ViBe; once you cross 95%, you're covered. Completing it elsewhere (not on ViBe with VLED) does not count — this exemption is only for courses completed with this programme; first-timers confident in their fundamentals can check the coursework email for the separate viva route. Exemptions are verified against ViBe and confirmed on your dashboard with no further action needed. Exemption form: https://forms.gle/RWt1v22yVePyZXD79 . Note: even if exempted from a course, live sessions and stand-ups remain mandatory for everyone, regardless of exemption status — no exceptions.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: How do I register for the AI Fundamentals course on Vibe?
faqs.answer: (1) Click the AI Fundamentals registration link posted in the Announcements section on samagama.in at Phase 1 launch. (2) You'll be redirected to the Vibe sign-in page — if you don't have a Vibe account yet, create one using the same Gmail you used to register on Samagama. (3) Log in with your credentials. (4) After logging in, open the course registration link again in your browser — the second click after login is what enrols you. (5) Complete the brief registration form and submit. (6) The course will appear instantly on your Vibe dashboard, ready to watch.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: I registered on Vibe with a different email than my Samagama email — is that OK?
faqs.answer: Use the same email on both platforms so Phase 1 progress can be matched to your internship record. The one exception: Vibe requires a Gmail address for signup — if your Samagama email is not Gmail (e.g. a college email), you may use any Gmail of yours to register on Vibe. In that case, tell Yaksha in your Samagama chat using the tag: #vibe-email your-gmail@gmail.com so the records can be linked.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: Are live sessions mandatory if I'm on the viva route?
faqs.answer: Yes — live sessions are mandatory for every intern, regardless of path. Whether on the coursework track, MERN-exempt (returning intern), or cleared the viva and moved to Phase 2, every intern is expected to attend every live session, since the exchange of knowledge across the cohort — diverse streams, varying levels of experience — is something self-paced study cannot replicate.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: When and how do I get the Zoom link for the kickoff meeting?
faqs.answer: The kickoff orientation is held for the main summer cohort only — candidates starting at the opening of the May–July window. The Zoom link is delivered via email to your registered samagama.in address, and through your Yaksha chat portal (log in, open the chat, and the link is shown there). If your start date is later (mid-summer or beyond), there is no separate kickoff event for you. If you cannot register with the Zoom link or have not received it, log in and type #escalate in the chat with Yaksha.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: How do I get the link for the daily Zoom standups? Are they mandatory?
faqs.answer: Daily Zoom standup links are posted in the Announcements section on your samagama.in dashboard (look for the announcement bell at the top) — check it daily before the session; links are posted at least 1 hour before sessions begin. No separate emails are sent for daily standups; the dashboard announcement is the only delivery channel. Attending daily standups is mandatory for all interns — this is a full-time summer internship, and the standup is the primary touchpoint for progress, blockers, and the day's plan; missing standups is treated as missing work, and attendance/participation are tracked against strict thresholds. The kickoff orientation was held for the recommended 15 May cohort, and recordings are not shared with interns who join late. If you joined late, you're expected to complete orientation through a special proctored catch-up path on ViBe, including quizzes checking your understanding — completing this catch-up is mandatory for late starters before joining regular standups.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: How do I provide my Zoom ID, and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: On your dashboard, just before 'Start the internship,' you'll see a step called 'Provide your Zoom ID.' Enter the exact email address linked to your Zoom account — the one you use (or will use) to join the daily live sessions — and save it. This matters because live-session attendance and participation are matched using this email; if it doesn't match the email you actually join Zoom with, your attendance won't be credited.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: I saved the wrong Zoom ID — can I change it?
faqs.answer: Once saved, your Zoom ID is final and cannot be changed by you — double-check before submitting. If you're certain you entered the wrong email, log in and tell the team in the chat (type #escalate) with your correct Zoom email, and it will be reviewed and corrected for you.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: Can we register and start the vibe courses before our internship date formally starts?
faqs.answer: You will receive the ViBe course link only after your internship starts. You can register and start the ViBe courses related to the internship only after your internship formally starts.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: What are the attendance and participation rules?
faqs.answer: Attendance and participation are tracked strictly, measured continuously over a rolling window of the last 5 working days, across three requirements: Live-session attendance — at least 85% of the total Zoom meeting time; Live participation — responding to in-session polls and quizzes at least 85% of the times they are run; Quizzes — every quiz must be attempted, with a pass percentage of at least 50%. Because this is a rolling 5-working-day measure, it reflects recent engagement, not a one-time average. If any one of the three falls below its threshold, you will be excused from the current batch and moved to the next batch — this is not a penalty, simply a practical step so you can rejoin when you can give the programme full attention.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: What are Spurti Points (SP)? Do they affect my internship?
faqs.answer: Spurti Points are a platform feature that tracks your engagement with the programme. They are currently in an early beta phase and are not used for any decisions about your standing. See the Spurti Points section for the full SP FAQ.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: What are the live-session (Zoom) participation and conduct rules?
faqs.answer: Treat every live session as a professional classroom; repeated non-compliance may result in removal from a session. Video & visibility: keep video on at all times unless explicitly permitted otherwise (participants with video off will be removed); face must be clearly visible — well-lit, centred, not rotated or cut off; do not join from a mobile phone, use a laptop or desktop. Audio & environment: test your microphone and video using the platform's audio-test feature before the session, especially if you plan to speak; be in a reasonably quiet, well-lit space. Conduct & attire: dress code is business casuals or neat Indian casuals — clothing inappropriate for a professional/classroom setting is not allowed; no multitasking — talking on the phone or with people nearby, or any distraction/disengagement, may result in removal. Display name & profile picture: display your full name (first and last) — nicknames or partial names are not acceptable; a professional, clearly identifiable profile picture is strongly recommended (enforced starting the next session); use your professional email account to join wherever applicable. Breaks & reconnection: for a short break, indicate 'BRB' and return promptly — prolonged absence may result in a permanent ban; breaks are provided periodically, manage personal needs then; if your internet drops and you're moved to the waiting room, you will not be re-admitted, so ensure a stable connection (ideally with a backup) before joining. Waiting room: do not call or message the Core Team for re-admission from the waiting room — doing so will result in an SP deduction. Speaking effectively: keep responses concise (two to three sentences), rehearse before unmuting, and test your mic/video beforehand if you plan to speak. Grounds for removal or a permanent ban: video off, joining from mobile, poor visibility, unprofessional attire, disengagement, extended unexplained absence, non-compliance with display-name/picture rules, or repeated violations of session guidelines.

faqs.category: Phase 1 — coursework, Vibe LMS, and live sessions
faqs.question: I got "Failed to submit poll. Error: 100035000" during a session — what does it mean, and will I lose poll credit?
faqs.answer: This error appears when the poll closed before your response was submitted — either the timer ran out or there was a brief lag between your client and the host. During the session: don't panic or leave the meeting; if the poll window is still visible, wait a few seconds and try again; if the error persists and the poll has closed, note the time and session date for when you report it. To reduce the chance of this happening: submit your response as soon as the poll appears, stay attentive throughout the session, keep a backup internet connection ready (e.g. mobile hotspot), keep your Zoom app updated, and use the Zoom desktop or mobile app rather than the browser, which is significantly less reliable for polls.

faqs.question: What are Spurti Points?
faqs.answer: Spurti Points, or SP, are a points layer on the platform that reflects your overall engagement with the programme. Think of SP as an indicator of your engagement — nothing more; it is not a score that defines you as a student or determines your future in the programme.

faqs.question: Is SP a finished system?
faqs.answer: No. Spurti Points are still being actively built and refined — best understood as a work in progress rather than a finalised grading or evaluation system. The rules and calculations may evolve as the programme develops.

faqs.question: How much importance should I give to my SP number?
faqs.answer: Please do not read too much into the number. SP is an early beta feature meant to give a broad sense of your engagement, not to measure your performance or determine outcomes.

faqs.question: Can I be terminated or excused because of low SP?
faqs.answer: No. The programme team will not terminate, excuse, or take any decision about any intern on the basis of Spurti Points alone. SP is not used as a basis for such decisions.

faqs.question: What if my SP shows as zero or even negative?
faqs.answer: There is genuinely no cause for concern. A zero or negative SP balance does not mean you are in trouble with the programme. Because SP is in an early beta phase, the number may not always reflect your actual effort or attendance accurately.

faqs.question: Does a higher SP bring any benefits?
faqs.answer: Yes, in a positive way — interns who build up higher Spurti Points may, from time to time, become eligible for small perks or recognition from the programme team. Think of a higher SP as a pleasant upside to aim for; it is not used to punish a low number. The one exception is that a mentor may deduct SP for clearly non-compliant behaviour (e.g. disrupting a live session or ignoring session conduct rules) — a deliberate response to conduct, not the same as the beta number simply being low.

faqs.question: If SP does not determine outcomes, what does?
faqs.answer: Your attendance and live participation are what the programme watches closely and tracks strictly. A low SP number is not a cue to ease off — the programme has clear participation requirements monitored independently of SP (see the attendance and participation rules FAQ for the exact thresholds).

faqs.question: What are the participation requirements tracked strictly?
faqs.answer: Looking at your most recent five working days on a rolling basis, every intern is expected to meet all three of: staying present for at least 85% of total live Zoom session time; responding to at least 85% of polls and quizzes run during sessions; and attempting every quiz with a score of at least 50%. All three requirements apply simultaneously — falling short on even one counts.

faqs.question: What does "rolling basis" mean?
faqs.answer: The programme does not look at a single average across the entire duration. Instead, it looks at your most recent five working days at any given point — as each new working day passes, the oldest day drops out and the newest is added. This means recent, consistent engagement counts, not a strong performance in one week followed by long absences; the window keeps moving forward.

faqs.question: What happens if I fall below the required participation level?
faqs.answer: If any one of the three participation requirements slips below the mark across your most recent five working days, you will be moved from the current batch into a later batch. This is not a termination or excusal from the programme — it's a practical step so you can rejoin when able to give full attention. To rejoin, you must upload revised documents: update your internship dates on the dashboard to match your new batch window, and upload a fresh NOC from your HOD covering the revised period (dates must match the dashboard exactly). Your previous NOC and confirmed dates are tied to the batch you were excused from and cannot be carried forward; a new offer letter is issued once revised documents are verified.

faqs.question: How are Spurti Points calculated?
faqs.answer: Every intern starts with a base of 100 SP credited on their official internship start date. SP is then earned through two live-session activities — attendance and poll participation — scored independently for every mandatory morning session. Attendance: 90%+ time present earns +10 SP; 75–89% earns +5 SP; 50–74% earns +3 SP; below 50% earns 0 SP. Poll participation: 90%+ polls answered earns +10 SP; 75–89% earns +5 SP; 50–74% earns +3 SP; below 50% earns 0 SP. Both are scored per session and summed across all mandatory sessions attended since your start date. There is no penalty for absence — missing a session earns 0 for that day, not a deduction. Only mandatory morning standup/orientation sessions count; breakouts, weekend specials, and evening sessions do not contribute. SP is updated daily, with current balance and full transaction history visible on samagama.in. Important: SP is a reward and recognition feature — it is not used by the programme team to judge engagement and plays no role in excusal decisions; excusal is determined from raw Zoom attendance logs, independently of SP. A low or zero SP number has no bearing on your standing in the programme.

faqs.question: Can the programme team award or deduct SP directly?
faqs.answer: Yes. In addition to the automatic daily rubric, programme team members can manually award or deduct Spurti Points at their discretion — to recognise good behaviour (helping peers, exceptional contributions, going above and beyond), to acknowledge cohort-protocol adherence (session etiquette, forum norms, community guidelines), or to apply conduct deductions (a deliberate response to disruptive or non-compliant behaviour, such as repeatedly violating session conduct rules or pestering the core team unnecessarily). Every manual award or deduction is permanently logged in the SP ledger with the awarding team member's identity and a justification note, visible in your SP transaction history. This does not change the excusal threshold — excusal is determined solely from raw Zoom attendance logs, and a manual SP deduction cannot lead to excusal; it is a recognition/conduct signal, not a standing decision.

faqs.question: I'm unable to type in the chat after clicking 'Interact with Yaksha' — what should I do?
faqs.answer: The chat input is only active after you have clicked the 'Interact with Yaksha' button. If you are still unable to type, scroll up to the top of the page — the button may be above the visible area. Click it once, and the chat field will become active.

faqs.question: How do I log in to ViBe?
faqs.answer: Website link: https://vibe.vicharanashala.ai/auth — sign up as a student with your registered mail ID. To log in: (1) log in to the ViBe platform as a student from your registered email ID; (2) check the 'Notifications' tab in the top right of the dashboard; (3) accept the course invite sent for your respective MERN course. Note: logging in with a different email ID may result in access issues or missing course visibility.

faqs.question: Invite accepted but shows "No course enrolled"?
faqs.answer: First, make sure you're logged in with the correct registered email ID; check if your college email has multiple aliases and try the correct one; log out and log in again once; use personal wifi instead of college wifi, as there may be network restrictions. If the issue continues: Step 1 — Allow Third-Party Cookies: open chrome://settings/cookies, turn OFF 'Block third-party cookies' and turn ON 'Allow all cookies'; add a site exception for .vicharanashala.ai with 'Including third-party cookies' enabled; restart the browser. Step 2 — Fix DNS: change your laptop's DNS to Google DNS (Control Panel → Network → Active Network → Properties → IPv4, or Win+R → ncpa.cpl → right-click properties); set Preferred DNS to 8.8.8.8 and Alternate DNS to 8.8.4.4; save. Step 3 — Flush old DNS cache: open Command Prompt as Admin and run ipconfig /flushdns, ipconfig /release, ipconfig /renew; restart WiFi.

faqs.question: Why are videos stuck or repeating?
faqs.answer: This may happen due to ViBe's monitored learning system. Common reasons: videos must be watched fully and in sequence (no skipping); camera and microphone permissions must be enabled; poor lighting or high background noise can affect playback; switching tabs or staying idle may restart the video. For smooth playback, stay on the ViBe tab, keep your camera on, and watch videos in a quiet, well-lit environment.

faqs.question: Can I use a mobile or tablet?
faqs.answer: No, only desktop/laptop is supported.

faqs.question: I'm experiencing video issues (stuck, looping, skipping) on ViBe. How do I troubleshoot?
faqs.answer: Try these steps in order: (1) refresh the page and check multiple times; (2) inspect the browser console (right-click → Inspect → Network or Console tab) and try watching the video to check for visible errors; (3) log out and log in again; (4) use a different browser; (5) clear browsing data and cache, then re-login. If the issue persists after all steps, record the issue and contact Yaksha, mentioning #escalate-ViBe.

faqs.question: I have completed all videos and quizzes in the ViBe course, but my progress is still showing less than 100%. What should I do?
faqs.answer: This might be a skip in the quiz/video item due to a penalty score, where the item may not have been successfully completed/marked. Verify you've completed all course items (1006/1006); if not, retry the missed content. In the meantime, try refreshing your browser, or logging out, clearing your browser cache, and logging in again.

faqs.question: I feel the ViBe content or platform is not good or I am unhappy with the way progress is evaluated. Can I request an exception or bypass the system?
faqs.answer: ViBe is built and continuously improved by interns and students themselves — it is a free and open-source learning platform, and the goal is to keep it that way by encouraging the community to contribute, improve, and strengthen it rather than bypass it. If a learner strongly feels the regular ViBe flow doesn't fairly reflect their understanding, there is a formal alternative evaluation path, intentionally rigorous to ensure fairness: watch the specified YouTube video content completely (links provided), then appear for a three-hour proctored examination based only on that content, written under strict supervision with two cameras (front and side view) and one online human proctor monitoring live. This exam becomes the sole basis for evaluation in place of the regular internship track. Scoring rules: below 60% — not qualified, must join the next cohort and continue only through normal ViBe mode; 60–80% — one more chance in the next scheduled exam; above 80% — considered passed. This special exam is conducted once every fortnight, so this route may significantly delay progress compared to continuing normally on ViBe — most students find the standard ViBe workflow faster and better.

faqs.question: Is the ViBe consent form compulsory? What if I don't want to grant camera access?
faqs.answer: Yes — the consent form is compulsory. Providing consent is mandatory for any candidate enrolling in and continuing a course on the ViBe Learning Platform. The platform is designed with proctoring enabled throughout, requiring access to your webcam and microphone, essential for fairness across participants, academic integrity, and active and genuine participation. If you choose not to grant camera and microphone access, you cannot proceed with the course, as proctoring is integral to the learning and evaluation workflow. Privacy & monitoring clarification: ViBe does not continuously record videos; proctoring operates via real-time monitoring mechanisms during learning and assessments; all data is handled strictly according to the stated consent terms and applicable data-protection guidelines.

faqs.question: What are penalty scores on the ViBe platform, and how do they affect our performance or HP?
faqs.answer: Penalty scores are generated when anomalies are detected during your activity on the platform (e.g. irregular behavior while watching video lessons or attempting quizzes). If the penalty score becomes high, you may be required to rewatch the video lesson and retake the associated quiz. At present, penalty scores do not impact your HP (Health Points) or overall performance evaluation, as they are not considered for scoring — their primary purpose is to ensure proper engagement with the learning content.

faqs.question: When should I use the Flag option on ViBe, and when should I contact support?
faqs.answer: Use the Flag feature only for course content-related issues, such as problems with video content or quiz questions. For technical issues, platform errors, login problems, or logistics-related queries, do not use the flag option — contact Yaksha so the team can assist faster. Using the correct method helps resolve issues quickly and keeps the learning process smooth for everyone.

faqs.question: What is Linear Progression on ViBe?
faqs.answer: Linear progression is enabled for every course on ViBe — each learner must watch videos and attempt quizzes in the exact order they appear on the left panel. In practice: you cannot click on a video or quiz far ahead of your current position; you must complete each item before the next unlocks; skipping is not allowed by design. Progress is sequential — finish the item in front of you, and the next one opens automatically.

faqs.question: Can I use the left navigation panel to jump ahead to a later video or quiz?
faqs.answer: No. Although the left navigation panel shows the full list of course items, it is meant only as a progress map, not a jump-to-anywhere menu. Instead, click 'Next Quiz' or 'Next Lesson' on the right panel and let the platform unlock items in sequence as you complete each one. Trying to skip ahead through the left panel will simply trigger the Access Restricted alert.

faqs.question: I am seeing a red "Access Restricted" banner. Is this a bug?
faqs.answer: No, this is not a bug — it's an intentional alert. The banner is a red toast notification with an exclamation icon, the title 'Access Restricted', and the message 'Returning to previous valid content.' It appears when you try to open an item before completing all items that come before it. ViBe automatically returns you to the last item you legitimately reached — you will not lose any progress, you'll simply be sent back to where you actually are in the course. It is the system signalling that there is something earlier in the course you haven't finished yet.

faqs.question: How do I resolve the "Access Restricted" error?
faqs.answer: Follow these steps in order: (1) go back to the left panel and scroll through your course items from the beginning; (2) look for any item without a completion tick — that's your missed video or quiz; (3) complete that item (watch the full video, or attempt and submit the quiz); (4) refresh the page once all earlier items are completed; (5) if the banner still appears after refreshing and you're sure all earlier items are done, report the issue to Yaksha mentioning #escalate-ViBe. In the large majority of cases, simply finding and completing the missed item clears the alert.

faqs.question: Why does ViBe sometimes make me re-watch a clip after a quiz?
faqs.answer: If your answer to the check-in quiz didn't go through correctly, ViBe takes you back to the same clip to try again — this is called a re-watch and is part of the design, not a penalty. Re-watches are not recorded against you and do not affect your HP or evaluation; clips are short, so a re-watch usually costs less time than guessing through multiple retries. Think of it as the platform helping the idea stick before you move on.

faqs.question: What kinds of quiz questions will I see on ViBe?
faqs.answer: ViBe quizzes come in four formats: Pick one (MCQ) — one right answer out of four or so options, read all options before clicking; Pick one or more (MSQ) — 'select all that apply', could be one or several correct options, read each option carefully as small mistakes happen here most; Type a number (NAT) — a text box asking for a numeric value, type just the number with no units or extra symbols unless the question explicitly asks; True or False — a statement with only two options, read carefully as a single word like 'always,' 'never,' or 'only' can flip the correct answer. Tip: watch the clip first, then answer — trying to read the question while the clip is still playing splits your attention.

faqs.question: Are the same proctoring rules applied to every course on ViBe?
faqs.answer: No. ViBe's proctoring system is modular — each individual check (face visibility, single-face-in-frame, lighting, background voices, gaze on screen, camera/microphone access) can be independently switched on or off by the instructor for a given course or cohort. The instructor decides which proctoring elements are active; some courses run with all checks active (typical for internships, faculty FDPs, and credentialed programmes), others with only a subset, and certain pilot or open courses may have most proctoring relaxed where the focus is exploration rather than verified evaluation. The detailed proctoring FAQs are written as if everything is switched on — whether a specific check applies to your course depends entirely on what your instructor has enabled. A relaxation given in one course (e.g. allowing two faces in frame for a paired-learning module) does not transfer to another course. When in doubt, ask your instructor or programme coordinator which proctoring elements are active for your current course.

faqs.question: What does the "quiet helper" on ViBe actually do?
faqs.answer: While a lesson plays, a small helper runs gently on your device using your camera and microphone, checking in real time that basic conditions for learning are present. It looks at five things: a face is visible (confirming you're really there); only one face is in frame (confirming the learning is yours); there is enough light on your face (a silhouette is hard to recognise); the room isn't full of voices (no talking, TV, or background podcasts); and you are looking at the screen (brief glances away are fine; long stretches feel like drifting off). The helper is not a judge — brief, normal movements (a stretch, a sneeze, a glance at your notebook) are fine; it only pays attention to sustained patterns, not split-second things.

faqs.question: Does ViBe record long videos of me while I'm learning?
faqs.answer: No. ViBe does not continuously record videos of you — the camera and microphone are used for real-time presence checks only; long recordings of your face or voice are not stored; when the lesson ends, the helper goes quiet too. All data is handled strictly according to the consent terms shown when you signed up. Think of the helper as a quiet study partner sitting beside you — there if needed, invisible otherwise.

faqs.question: What is the single most common avoidable mistake learners make?
faqs.answer: Sitting with a window directly behind you during the day. The room may feel bright to you, but your camera only sees a dark silhouette where your face should be — the helper then struggles to confirm your presence, and your lesson may pause or rewind. The fix: move so the window is to your side or in front of the laptop, not behind you; a lamp placed in front of you works just as well in the evening.

faqs.question: Why does the lesson keep pausing or restarting even when I'm paying attention?
faqs.answer: If a clip keeps stopping or going back to the start, the cause is almost always something small in your environment, not the platform. Run through this checklist: your face is too dark (add a lamp in front of you); your face is partly out of frame (raise the laptop or sit closer to the camera); there are voices in the background (close the door or move to a quieter room); you switched tabs or went idle (stay on the ViBe tab, take breaks between clips, not during them); camera or mic permission dropped (check the lock icon in your browser's address bar and confirm both are set to 'Allow').

faqs.question: Can I read the quiz questions aloud or mutter to myself while watching?
faqs.answer: It's best not to. The microphone listens for sustained voices in the room, and reading aloud, muttering, or asking a roommate a question can all be picked up as anomalies. The simple habit is: watch in silence, answer in silence, and chat freely during your breaks between clips.

faqs.question: Can I study with a friend on camera since we're learning together?
faqs.answer: In most courses, no — only you, the registered Learner, should be in the camera frame during a lesson, since the helper checks that exactly one face is visible at a time. Group study is a wonderful habit, just not inside a ViBe session itself: hop on a separate call with your friend on phone or a second device, discuss concepts before or after a ViBe lesson, and return to ViBe alone when ready to watch the clip and attempt the quiz. Note: the single-face-in-frame check is an instructor-controlled setting, and a few courses (typically paired-learning or collaborative pilots) may explicitly relax this rule — do not assume a relaxation in one course applies to another, even on the same platform with the same login; always default to studying alone unless your instructor has clearly stated otherwise for that specific course.

faqs.question: Will I lose my progress if I clear my browser or reinstall it?
faqs.answer: No. Your progress is saved on the server, tied to your registered email — not on your browser or computer. Refreshing the page, clearing cache, switching browsers, or even reinstalling your browser will not wipe your progress; the moment you log back in with the same registered email, all completed clips and quizzes will be exactly where you left them. When in doubt: log out, log back in.

faqs.question: Is there a recommended daily learning rhythm on ViBe?
faqs.answer: Yes — small, regular sessions work far better than long marathon sessions. A practical rhythm: show up daily, even if only for thirty minutes (daily consistency beats a five-hour weekend cram); take breaks between clips, not during them; treat each quiz as a gentle check-in, not a high-stakes test; for programmes with deadlines (internships, faculty cohorts), aim for the daily progress target announced by your programme team — typically around 3.33% per day. Note: this pacing is a general guideline only — actual milestones, deadlines, and weekly progress targets for any specific programme will be announced by your instructors or programme team, and that schedule always takes precedence over this general guidance.

faqs.question: What should my "study corner" look like before I start a ViBe session?
faqs.answer: A ViBe-friendly study spot needs only three things: (1) light in front of your face — a lamp or window facing you, never behind; (2) just you in the camera frame — ask family, friends, or pets not to wander through; (3) a reasonably quiet room — no TV, no music with words, no one else on a call nearby. Two minutes of setup before you start saves a lot of small frustrations during the lesson.

faqs.question: I'm facing a technical issue on ViBe that I can't resolve. Is there live support available?
faqs.answer: Yes. A Live Support Breakout Session is held every day at 2:00 PM. If you're facing any platform issue on ViBe — videos not loading, progress not updating, access errors, login problems, or anything else you can't resolve on your own — join the breakout session and the support team will assist you in real time. This is the fastest way to get a ViBe issue resolved; please attempt the self-troubleshooting steps first, then bring the issue to the 2 PM session if it persists.

faqs.question: Is team formation compulsory?
faqs.answer: Yes. All projects in Phase 2 and Phase 3 (some parts) must be completed in teams. Every participant is required to be part of a team.

faqs.question: What is the size of a team?
faqs.answer: The team size is fixed at four members. This is mandatory — you cannot have fewer or more members in a team at the time of final formation.

faqs.question: How are teams formed?
faqs.answer: For students who joined on May 15 and 16: teams were formed through a structured activity. For students joining later: teams will be randomly assigned by the administration.

faqs.question: I started on May 15/16 but couldn't form a team during the activity. What happens now?
faqs.answer: You will be randomly assigned to a team.

faqs.question: There was a typo in our email addresses during team formation. Can we fix it?
faqs.answer: No action is required from your side. The administration will verify and match email IDs with names before finalizing and locking teams.

faqs.question: I formed a team with only two members. Will it be considered?
faqs.answer: No. Teams with fewer than four members will be expanded by adding additional members to make a final team of four.

faqs.question: What if a team member leaves or becomes ineligible during Phase 1?
faqs.answer: The administration will attempt to assign a replacement member. If no replacement is found, you may continue as a team of three. You must inform the admin immediately, or the change will not be recognized.

faqs.question: Can I form a team with someone from my own college?
faqs.answer: No. Teams must consist of members from different institutions to encourage networking. Exception: students from the same institution but different campuses/locations may be allowed.

faqs.question: Can I form a team with students from my IIT MBS cohort?
faqs.answer: No. You are encouraged to collaborate with participants outside your existing cohort.

faqs.question: Can we change our team name after submission?
faqs.answer: Yes, team names are tentative and can be changed. However, due to operational constraints, frequent changes are discouraged.

faqs.question: What if multiple teams choose the same name?
faqs.answer: Teams will be distinguished using suffixes (e.g., Team X-1, Team X-2, etc.).

faqs.question: What should I do if I face issues within my team?
faqs.answer: Report any concerns immediately to your assigned scholar/mentor. Maintaining a safe and respectful environment is a priority.

faqs.question: How will I know who my mentor is?
faqs.answer: Your mentor will be the scholar assigned to the project your team is working on.

faqs.question: When will I know my team details?
faqs.answer: Team details are announced in the Announcements section on samagama.in. Log in and check regularly during working hours.

faqs.question: I received a team list email but my name is not included. What should I do?
faqs.answer: Team announcements are phased, so your name may appear in a later list. If your entire cohort has moved to team activities and you are still unassigned, raise the issue on Yaksha or contact a scholar.

faqs.question: We selected Project X as our top priority but were assigned Project Y. Can we change it?
faqs.answer: No. Project assignments are final and cannot be changed. Allocation is done to ensure balanced distribution across projects.

faqs.question: I just started the internship. Can I form my own team now?
faqs.answer: No. For later cohorts, teams will be randomly assigned. Please wait for the official communication.

faqs.question: When do team activities begin?
faqs.answer: Team-based work begins in Phase 2. During Phase 1 (online coursework), you do not need to worry about team activities.

faqs.question: Can I request a specific teammate after teams are assigned?
faqs.answer: No. Team assignments are final and requests for changes are not entertained.

faqs.question: What happens if a team member is inactive or not contributing?
faqs.answer: You should report the issue to your mentor/scholar early. Prolonged inactivity may lead to administrative intervention.

faqs.question: Can I switch teams if there are conflicts?
faqs.answer: Team switches are not allowed except in exceptional, admin-approved cases involving serious concerns.

faqs.question: Will team performance affect individual evaluation?
faqs.answer: Yes. While some components may be individual, team deliverables are a key part of evaluation.

faqs.question: How will communication happen within teams?
faqs.answer: Teams self-organise internal coordination over LinkedIn or email only, limited to their own team members. WhatsApp is not encouraged for team coordination, and it is not permitted to create a team WhatsApp group (a four-person project team is still a 'subset of interns', which is prohibited under the communication channels policy — a group of that form, if reported, will lead to immediate termination of the internship). Official programme updates continue to come through the Announcements section on samagama.in and Yaksha chat.

faqs.question: What if I miss the team allocation announcement?
faqs.answer: All programme updates are posted in the Announcements section on samagama.in. Log in and check regularly during working hours — this is the only channel for official notifications.

faqs.question: Can a team be dissolved and reformed?
faqs.answer: No. Once finalized, teams are locked and cannot be dissolved.

faqs.question: What happens if I drop out of the internship?
faqs.answer: Your team will be adjusted accordingly, and the remaining members may continue as a team of three or receive a replacement.

faqs.question: Will we get time to get to know our teammates before Phase 2?
faqs.answer: Yes. There is typically a buffer period before Phase 2 where teams can connect and prepare.
